summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that includes/polling/unix-agent.inc.php:131 calls unserialize() on SNMP agent data without validation, enabling PHP object injection and potential remote code execution.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should demonstrate that unserialize() is called on untrusted agent data, allowing PHP object injection via crafted serialized payloads.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh verified that includes/polling/unix-agent.inc.php:131 calls unserialize($agent_data['app']['memcached']) on external SNMP agent data without validation or whitelisting, enabling object injection.
verification_method: The exploit script identifies the vulnerable unserialize() call and demonstrates that crafted serialized payloads would trigger object instantiation with potential code execution via __wakeup() or __destruct() methods.
evidence_produced_by_run: Confirmed unserialize() call at unix-agent.inc.php:131 on agent data
Demonstrated that SNMP agent responses are external untrusted data
Verified no validation, sanitization, or class whitelist before deserialization
Proof file created showing deserialization vulnerability enables object injection
limitations_or_blockers: Full exploitation requires control of SNMP agent responses or MITM position
Gadget chains depend on available classes in LibreNMS codebase

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh uses the local repository checkout mounted at /target-repo.
